_risk_level_doc: medium — multi-file lockstep changes (test-writer manifest contract reconciliation crosses 3 files, tool-policy addition is additive, .gitignore + git rm --cached is well-scoped). No security boundary changes; no pipeline/API code touched. Risk is concentrated in the manifest read-path change (qa.md Phase 5) but that path was non-functional in Cycle 1 (test-writer never dispatched) so behavioral risk is minimal.

requirement.original: Cycle 2 of spec-20260518-225715 — implement the remediation plan in Section 7 (P1+P2+P3 only; P4 deferred to Cycle 3 per earlier user decision in this session).
requirement.clarified: Implement the 9 remediation items in spec Section 7 (P1.1, P1.2, P2.3, P2.4, P2.5, P3.6, P3.7, P3.8, P3.9) plus the codex-supplied completeness extensions (CP_AGENTS registration, test-writer.md internal consistency fix, git rm --cached for tracked telemetry, doc-sync skip lists, Sub-step 12.0 elimination alongside 12.1, canary-safe.txt mktemp, precise Standard 6 exemption wording). P4 items explicitly excluded.
requirement.what: 9-item close-gate remediation (P1 workflow integrity unblockers, P2 cleanliness gitignore, P3 style violations) for spec-20260518-225715 Cycle 1 close-gate NO verdict.
requirement.why: Cycle 1's /close gate returned NO with 3 failure axes (test-writer not consumable, runtime telemetry leaking, 12 style violations). User wants the spec closed; Cycle 2 fixes the gate-blockers without re-implementing Cycle 1.

success_criteria: AC-01 through AC-13 all PASS (13 ACs covering 9 remediation items + their completeness extensions)
/close gate returns YES on Cycle 2 (no longer blocked on consumability / cleanliness / style)
Cycle 1's PASS verdicts on the 18 original ACs remain intact (Cycle 2 does not regress Cycle 1)
constraints: Test-writer addition to tool-policy MUST use policy-native fnmatch globs (*/...) consistent with existing role shape
Manifest contract reconciliation MUST pick Option B (per-task active_tests, global as index) — Option A is rejected per spec design
git rm --cached is authorized for runtime-telemetry files only (agent-scores.json, .lock, cp-state-*) — NOT for user-authored content
Standard 6 exemption MUST enumerate exempted classes, NOT be a generic 'Chinese is OK if user-facing'

out_of_path_observed_but_not_touched: docs/dev/INDEX.md, docs/dev/README.md, docs/dev/archive/ (200+ untracked artifacts pre-existing)

out_of_scope_observations.observation: B-IT3F-1: ba role allowed_write_path_prefixes already contains acceptance-criteria- (per policy_version 3); blast-radius-* path prefix may still be needed for the blast-radius-map.json BA writes. Deferred to Cycle 3 P4.
B-IT3F-4: SessionStart hooks emit stdout (echo/printf without >&2) polluting prompt context. Flagged advisory by canary-verify.sh. Deferred to Cycle 3 P4.
Phase 2 misses net-new untracked files (git diff --name-only HEAD only). Union with git ls-files --others --exclude-standard needed. Deferred to Cycle 3 P4.

Phase 5 trigger uses file-presence check; should mirror dispatch gate (complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level = high). Asymmetry could allow stale manifests to fire Phase 5. Deferred to Cycle 3 P4.
write-guard and git-privilege-guard checks only verify exec errors (rc>=126); no negative-payload depth like bash-safety. Fail-open if hook silently allows unsafe payload. Deferred to Cycle 3 P4.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Cycle 1 /close gate returned NO with 3 failure axes despite QA PASS on 18/18 ACs
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Cycle 1's ACs were structurally scoped (verify Section 5 component existence) but not consumability-scoped (verify the components actually work end-to-end in the harness) or cleanliness-scoped (verify diff introduces no leakage) or style-scoped (verify diff introduces no inspector findings). The /close gate enforces the broader criteria; without pre-encoded ACs covering them, QA could PASS while /close failed.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: d988d4a chore(docs): ship dev cycle 20260519-175339 + re-enable docs/dev (Cycle 1 delivery point)
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Cycle 1 acceptance criteria were focused on spec Section 5 design verification; the /close gate's Bullet 1 (consumability), Bullet 4 sub-iv (cleanliness), and inspector findings (style) operate as a separate quality layer not encoded in the AC JSON.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Successful QA + failing /close is the worst possible signal — it tells the orchestrator the spec is done while the gate says it's not. Cycle 2 must bridge the gap by encoding the gate criteria directly into its own AC set.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Cycle 1 dispatched 2026-05-19T13:24Z; QA PASS 2026-05-19T16:38Z; /close NO 2026-05-19T20:02Z; user authorized Cycle 2 in-session 2026-05-20; BA Cycle 2 dispatch 2026-05-20T22:14Z.

root_cause_analysis.located_source: All 16 affected files explicitly named in development_approach.files_to_modify; each matched at least one keyword from complaint_location_keywords; cross-validation passed.

git_bisect_result.reason: not a regression — this is a known-state remediation cycle following a specific close-gate verdict, not a 'used to work' regression

_diagnosis_layer_doc: Primary layer is L5 (infrastructure: tool-policy, .gitignore, hook agent enrollment) because the user-need is fundamentally about making the harness accept the test-writer agent + stop leaking runtime state. L4 (manifest contract reconciliation, Sub-step renumbering) and L3 (script edits) and L1 (Standard 6 exemption wording) are also present but L5 is the dominant.

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Runtime-create score file if missing — makes git rm --cached safe (next invocation re-creates skeleton)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: if [[ ! -f "${SCORES_FILE}" ]]; then cat <<'EOF' ... [段位: 熟练工匠]... EOF; exit 0; fi
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Neutral-header fallback when score file missing — makes git rm --cached safe
Version-bumped policy with audit trail; new test-writer role addition should respect the v3 contract (additive, not breaking)

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: You MUST NOT run if `complexity_tier < STANDARD` AND `risk_level != high`. If invoked under those conditions, return a no-op report and exit.
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Trigger gate — test-writer is a no-op for MICRO/SMALL low-risk cycles. P1.2 contract changes MUST preserve this gate.

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Exemption (verbatim user-binding quotes): Verbatim non-English user-binding quotes belong in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only ... Code/script comments and user-visible diagnostic strings ... must be English with task-id attribution
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Existing exemption clause — Cycle 2 adds a SECOND exemption, does NOT modify or weaken this one

pre_existing_guards.code_snippet: Sub-step 12.0 (apply spec section updates BEFORE decision tree) ... triggers apply_spec_section_updates workflow
pre_existing_guards.purpose: Procedural ordering guard — Sub-step 12.0 MUST execute before the decision tree in spec-driven cycles. Renaming the label preserves the procedural semantics; do NOT remove the conditional.

_regression_investigation_doc: Omitted: this is a known-state remediation cycle following Cycle 1's close-gate verdict, not a regression bug. No 'used to work' / 'broke after X' phrasing in the user requirement.

novelty_check.rationale: Cycle 1 added new components at L4 (logic) and inadvertently created L1 (style) + L5 (cleanliness/consumability) debt. Cycle 2 targets the L5 + L1 layers explicitly (tool-policy, hook agent enrollment, .gitignore, doc-sync, script style, agent-prompt exemption). Layer-differentiation requirement satisfied.

context.codebase_state: 327+ uncommitted files in working tree (mix of Cycle 1 delivery + parallel-session work + Cycle 2-pending). Cycle 1 delivery committed at d988d4a. Parallel session work on hooks/lib/allowlist.py + posttool-subagent-track.py + tests/test_allowlist_consolidation.py is a separate file set and does NOT overlap with Cycle 2 scope.
context.recent_commits: d988d4a (Cycle 1 ship), 34210cc (orphan unattributed), 6cd997b (tmpfs prevention), 8d74e83 (hook consent), bdf3604 (commit timestamp)

development_approach.strategy: Patch each of the 16 affected files (12 in user-need path + 4 path-dependent shared infra) per the 9 user-need items + 5 codex completeness extensions. Execute the `git rm --cached` step AFTER the .gitignore append (so the next git status correctly shows them as untracked). Re-run doc-sync regeneration LAST so it sees the updated skip lists. Verify each AC after the corresponding file edit.
development_approach.validation_approach: QA runs each of AC-01 through AC-13 via deterministic bash/grep/git/python commands per the executable AC JSON; for STANDARD-tier cycles per QA Phase 5 trigger, no pytest-generated tests apply here because this cycle's risk_level=medium and complexity_tier=STANDARD does not trigger test-writer (test-writer triggers on STANDARD+ AND risk_level>=high, OR any+high — this is STANDARD+medium, so test-writer is NOT dispatched). The blast-radius map will show the cycle's file-impact graph.

research_findings.reason: Straightforward remediation cycle with all prescriptive guidance already in spec Section 7 plus codex consultation (which serves as the adversarial review). No external best-practices research adds value when the cycle is bounded by an authoritative in-repo prescription.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh) returned 10 findings, all with PROPOSED_FIX. Findings #1, #3, #4, #5, #6, #7, #8, #9, #10 were applied (each expanded a Section 7 item into completeness — e.g., #3 added CP_AGENTS+prompt-workflow registration to P1.1 because tool-policy alone would not make test-writer dispatchable; #6 added git rm --cached to P2.3 because tracked files persist despite .gitignore; #8 expanded P3.7 from 12.1-only to 12.0+12.1 because both are decimal violations). Finding #2 was applied (policy-native path-prefix shape using */... fnmatch globs).

findings.issue: P1.1 policy write scope missing docs/dev/test-writer-report- path
findings.rationale: test-writer.md output contract writes to that path; without the prefix the agent's own report write would be policy-denied

findings.issue: P1.1 path patterns wrong shape for this policy engine
findings.proposed_fix: Use policy-native */... prefixes matching existing roles

findings.issue: P1.1 tool-policy alone insufficient for consumability (CP_AGENTS + prompt-workflow.py missing test-writer)
findings.proposed_fix: Add test-writer to hooks/prompt-workflow.py agent_types + hooks/pretool-cp-checkin.py CP_AGENTS
findings.rationale: AC-02 added to encode this requirement; cited as M-P1.1b in the Must-Have section

findings.issue: P1.2 test-writer.md procedure step 5 + report still reference global manifest as active
findings.proposed_fix: Make procedure step 5 + report consistent with per-task schema (writes per-task active manifest, maintains global as index)
findings.rationale: AC-03 added with must_not_contain to catch the residual drift

findings.issue: P1.2 QA changes must include pytest scope (currently `pytest tests/generated/ -q` recursive) + output schema
findings.proposed_fix: Scope pytest to tests/generated/<task_id>/; update schema field too
findings.rationale: AC-04 expanded to cover both Phase 5 procedure and output schema

findings.issue: P2.3 .gitignore alone does not untrack tracked files
findings.proposed_fix: Add git rm -r --cached for agent-scores.json, agent-scores.json.lock, .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715
findings.rationale: M-P2.3b + AC-07 added; user-authorization chain documented in Edge Cases & Risks

findings.issue: P2.4 regen-only insufficient — doc-sync filesystem-scan ignores .gitignore
findings.proposed_fix: Augment doc-sync SKIP_FILES/IGNORE_DIRS to exclude leaked patterns

findings.issue: P3.7 Sub-step 12.1 only is incomplete — Sub-step 12.0 also decimal
findings.rationale: AC-11 expanded to cover both labels

findings.issue: P3.8 /tmp/canary-oversized only is incomplete — /tmp/canary-safe.txt on line 79 same issue
findings.proposed_fix: mktemp both safe and oversized files
findings.rationale: AC-12 expanded to cover both literals

findings.proposed_fix: Enumerate exempted classes (rank labels, AskUserQuestion choices); re-affirm code comments + stderr remain English
findings.rationale: AC-13 requires precise class enumeration + must_not_contain guard against generic phrasing

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 10 codex findings applied — each expanded a Section 7 item into the completeness needed for the gate to actually clear. No findings rejected; no observation-only entries.
